calibration_history.question: When you're on the home page, do you scroll down into the gallery rows regularly, or is the hero + UP NEXT the main area you interact with?
calibration_history.prediction: Hero + UP NEXT is primary; gallery rows are secondary/backup discovery
calibration_history.actual: Scrolls into gallery rows regularly, uses thumbs up/down actively as they pass cards
calibration_history.insight: Gallery rows are active daily usage, not backup discovery. Thumbs ratings UI on every row is essential — missing it (e.g. Top10Row) is a real friction point.

calibration_history.question: When you're watching a video and the suggestions rail is visible, do you use it to pick your next video, or do you navigate back to home?
calibration_history.prediction: Goes back to home most of the time; rail is secondary discovery
calibration_history.actual: Hover previews ARE the watch experience — watch page is the commit destination of a preview-dwell. Doesn't really browse the suggestions rail.
calibration_history.insight: Watch page is an arrival point, not a browse surface. The suggestions rail is low-priority chrome.

calibration_history.question: Do you watch on desktop at your desk or from a distance (couch, standing)?
calibration_history.actual: Home = desktop (hover), feed = mobile + TV (touch/remote, no hover). Surface-by-device split is real.
calibration_history.insight: Home page is designed for hover/desktop. Feed is for mobile and TV. Different affordance requirements per surface.

calibration_history.question: Do you use the Remix mode in the feed, or is it dead weight?
calibration_history.actual: Remix is dead weight — For You only; Remix can be deprecated.
calibration_history.insight: Remix is unused. For You is the only feed mode that matters.

taste_notes: Preview-first watching: hover previews ARE the watch experience. The watch page is the commit destination of a preview-dwell transition — not a browse surface.
Surface-by-device split: home = desktop (hover/mouse), feed = mobile + TV (touch/remote). Different affordance requirements.
Remix is dead weight: For You is the only feed mode that matters. Remix can be deprecated.
Restraint pays: PosterPeekRow removal was a net Pillar 3 win. Subtraction is as valid as addition.
Titles are secondary to thumbnails on browse surfaces — Torin doesn't read card titles when scanning.
Gallery rows are active daily usage: Torin scrolls into them regularly and uses thumbs up/down as he passes. Ratings UI on every row is essential, not optional chrome.
UP NEXT spam: recently the UP NEXT carousel has been showing spam/low-quality content — content quality filter needs attention.
